claims.text: Patient 1 (female) carries c.1935C>A (p.D645E) as a heterozygous maternally-inherited pathogenic variant in compound heterozygosity with c.1669A>T (p.I557F) inherited from the father; the variant is classified as pathogenic.

citations.quote:
1 | exon12 | c.1669A > T | p.I557F | het | paternal | pathogenic |
| | exon14 | c.1935C > A | p.D645E | het | maternal | pathogenic |

claims.text: Patient 8 (male) carries c.1935C>A (p.D645E) as a homozygous variant inherited from both parents; the variant is classified as pathogenic.

citations.quote: 8 | exon14 | c.1935C > A | p.D645E | hom | parental | pathogenic |

claims.text: Patient 1 (female, BSA 0.35 m²) had MLVWT 22 mm, LVPW 15 mm, LVOT gradient 1 mmHg, LVEF 54%, E/e′ ratio 15.8, and GLS 9%, consistent with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
Patient 8 (male, BSA 0.31 m²) had MLVWT 13 mm, LVPW 7 mm, LVOT gradient 3 mmHg, LVEF 48%, E/e′ ratio 26.7, and GLS 7.6%, consistent with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

The paper identifies two patients (one compound heterozygous, one homozygous) carrying c.1935C>A (p.D645E), classified as pathogenic in the context of infantile-onset Pompe disease in a Chinese cohort of 15 patients who all died at a median age of 24.80 months due to cardiorespiratory failure.

citations.quote: Fifteen patients died at a median age of 24.80 months due to cardiorespiratory failure (range 3–120 months). Myasthenia symptoms and severe hypertrophic cardiomyopathy were universally present (15/15 = 100%).

claims.text: The variant c.1935C>A (p.D645E) is cited as accounting for 80% of mutant alleles and being the most common mutation in Thai patients with infantile-onset Pompe disease, referencing Amarinthnukrowh et al.

citations.quote: In Thai patients, Amarinthnukrowh et al. reported that mutation c.1935C > A accounted for 80% of the mutant alleles and is the most common mutation in the Thai population (7).

claims.text: The paper's discussion references p.Asp645Glu (attributed to c.1C>A, likely a typographic error for c.1935C>A) as the most common mutation in patients from southern China, accounting for 20–25% of total mutant alleles.

citations.quote: The most common mutation in patients from southern China was c.1C > A (p.Asp 645Glu), accounting for 20%–25% of the total mutant alleles (20).

claims.text: The cohort's median age at symptom onset was 5.07 months (range 1–24 months) and median age at diagnosis was 19.53 months (range 3–109 months), applicable to the patients carrying c.1935C>A.

citations.quote: the median age of 15 patients at symptom onset was 5.07 months (1–24 months). The median age at diagnosis was 19.53 months (range: 3 to 109 months, n = 15).
